us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Maine Democrat Graham Platner says he will withdraw from Senate race
bullets: Platner said he plans to leave the U.S. Senate race after a sexual assault allegation.
His exit leaves Democrats scrambling to replace him before state deadlines.
Maine is viewed as a key Senate battleground in the 2026 midterms.

articles.title: Trump’s proposed triumphal arch in Washington faces another federal review
bullets: A federal commission is reviewing President Trump’s plan for a large arch in the capital.
Agency staff said the project should be revised before approval.
The proposal was displayed during America’s 250th anniversary events in Washington.

articles.title: Trump says he will seek Supreme Court reconsideration on birthright citizenship
bullets: Trump said he would ask the Supreme Court to revisit a ruling against his birthright-citizenship order.
The move was described by legal observers as a long-shot effort.
The issue remains central to litigation over Trump administration immigration policy.

articles.title: ACA marketplace premiums are projected to rise sharply again in 2027
bullets: A new analysis found insurers are proposing another year of double-digit premium increases.
Middle-income consumers who buy Affordable Care Act plans would be among those affected.
The findings follow large premium increases already seen this year.

articles.title: Trump post about St. Paul kindergartners sparks safety concerns
bullets: Community leaders criticized a Trump Truth Social post featuring a St. Paul kindergarten ceremony.
They said the post came amid rising anti-Muslim and anti-Somali rhetoric.
Local officials and families raised concerns about student safety and harassment.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Top worldwide stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Trump says interim Iran ceasefire is over as U.S. and Iran trade strikes
bullets: Trump said the interim arrangement to end the Iran war was over.
U.S. Central Command said fresh strikes targeted Iran’s ability to threaten Gulf shipping.
The escalation renewed fears over the Strait of Hormuz and global oil supplies.

articles.title: Iran says it struck U.S. military targets in the Gulf after U.S. attacks
bullets: Iran said its armed forces targeted U.S. military infrastructure in Gulf states.
The strikes followed U.S. attacks on Iran’s southern coastal and eastern provinces.
The escalation further strained a three-week-old ceasefire agreement.

articles.title: Putin likely to escalate Ukraine war despite Trump peace push, Reuters sources say
bullets: Three sources close to the Kremlin told Reuters Putin is rejecting calls to negotiate peace.
Two sources said Russia is likely to escalate the war in coming months.
Ukraine’s recent strikes on Russian oil infrastructure have hardened Moscow’s stance, the sources said.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill Palestinians in Gaza amid ceasefire violations, Anadolu reports
bullets: Gaza health officials reported deaths and injuries from Israeli attacks over the past 24 hours.
Images showed residents responding after a drone strike on a civilian vehicle in Gaza City.
The reported strikes came amid continued accusations of ceasefire violations.

articles.title: Trump dominates NATO summit as allies debate Iran and Ukraine support
bullets: The NATO summit in Ankara ended with Trump threatening a hard response to Iran.
Le Monde reported that Trump also met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
Allies sought to balance Ukraine commitments with rising Middle East tensions.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Southwest monsoon covers all of India after slowest advance since 2021
bullets: The IMD said the monsoon covered the entire country on July 9.
This year’s full coverage came one day later than the normal date.
July rainfall helped offset a dry June and bring seasonal totals back near normal.

articles.title: Heavy rain floods Delhi-NCR roads and triggers traffic disruptions
bullets: Delhi-NCR saw flooded roads, uprooted trees and traffic jams after heavy showers.
NDTV reported Delhi’s air quality improved to a satisfactory AQI reading after the rain.
Authorities warned of further weather disruptions in parts of the region.

articles.title: Modi meets Australian PM Albanese in Melbourne to discuss Indo-Pacific ties
bullets: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit focused on trade, investment and strategic cooperation.
The agenda included critical minerals, supply chains and Indo-Pacific cooperation.
The meeting came during Modi’s six-day, three-nation tour.

articles.title: India urges de-escalation after fresh U.S.-Iran confrontation
bullets: India called for de-escalation amid renewed U.S.-Iran strikes and shipping risks.
The foreign ministry warned that attacks on commercial shipping threaten energy security.
Officials emphasized uninterrupted energy flows and regional stability.

articles.title: ED freezes TMC bank deposits worth ₹440 crore under PMLA
bullets: The Enforcement Directorate froze bank deposits linked to the Trinamool Congress.
Indian Express reported the value at about ₹440 crore.
The ED probe cited transfers involving AITC accounts and aviation-related entities.

articles.title: Nvidia rises after report China may allow limited H200 chip purchases
bullets: Reuters reported China may let top AI firms buy limited Nvidia H200 chips.
Alibaba, ByteDance and DeepSeek were named among companies that could receive permission.
Nvidia shares rose after the report, according to the article.

articles.title: Broadcom stays in focus after Apple commits more than $30 billion to chip deal
bullets: Apple plans to spend more than $30 billion under a Broadcom chip-supply agreement.
The deal includes U.S.-made wireless components and a Colorado factory expansion.
Broadcom’s role as a key Apple supplier is extended through 2031.

articles.title: S&P 500 falls after Trump says Iran deal is over
bullets: The S&P 500 closed lower after Trump said the interim Iran deal was over.
Reuters said Broadcom led gains among recently battered chip stocks.
Geopolitical risk and energy-price concerns weighed on sentiment.

articles.title: Kura Sushi USA shares watched after fiscal Q3 results
bullets: Kura Sushi USA reported fiscal third-quarter 2026 revenue of $85.9 million.
The company reported basic EPS of $0.03 and net income of $0.4 million.
The earnings update put the restaurant stock back in focus for investors.

articles.title: Meta stock in focus as Reuters reports in-house AI chip production plan
bullets: Reuters reported Meta plans to start manufacturing its Iris AI chip in September.
The company aims to double computing capacity to 14 gigawatts next year.
The plan is meant to reduce reliance on Nvidia and AMD GPUs over time.

articles.title: Apple signs $30B-plus Broadcom deal for U.S.-made wireless chips
bullets: Apple signed a multiyear deal with Broadcom for more than 15 billion custom chips.
The agreement includes a $1.5 billion capital investment to expand a Colorado facility.
The deal is part of Apple’s broader U.S. investment commitment.

articles.title: Blue Origin reportedly raising $10 billion at $130 billion valuation
bullets: Blue Origin is reportedly seeking outside capital for the first time.
The reported round values Jeff Bezos’ rocket company at about $130 billion.
Fortune said the raise would help fund competition with SpaceX and NASA-contract work.

articles.title: Meta to invest over $9.1 billion in first Canadian AI data center
bullets: Meta said it will build its first AI data center in Canada.
The Alberta project is expected to be Meta’s largest data center outside the U.S.
The facility will be powered by a natural gas-fired plant tied to the Greenlight Electricity Center.

articles.title: FTC and states settle with Deere in right-to-repair case
bullets: The FTC and five states secured a settlement with Deere & Company.
The case alleged Deere limited farmers’ and independent shops’ ability to repair equipment.
The settlement requires Deere to provide repair tools and software access for 10 years.

articles.title: Kura Sushi USA reports fiscal third-quarter results
bullets: Kura Sushi USA announced fiscal Q3 2026 results on July 7.
Revenue rose to $85.9 million, according to earnings coverage.
Investors focused on profitability as the chain continued expanding locations.

articles.title: OpenAI releases GPT-Live voice models for more natural conversations
bullets: OpenAI released GPT-Live-1 and GPT-Live-1 mini.
The models are designed for more natural live voice interactions and better turn-taking.
TechCrunch reported they can route tasks to newer text models while conversation continues.

articles.title: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5 for coding and agentic tasks
bullets: SpaceXAI introduced Grok 4.5 on July 8.
The company described it as its smartest model for coding, agentic tasks and knowledge work.
The model was compared with frontier rivals on terminal and coding benchmarks.

articles.title: Meta explores ‘super sensing’ smart glasses, Fortune reports
bullets: Fortune cited reporting that Meta is exploring glasses that capture audio and images nearly continuously.
The concept has raised internal privacy debates around non-wearers and data handling.
The report followed Meta’s move to prevent camera use if the privacy light is tampered with.

articles.title: Meta plans September production for in-house Iris AI chip
bullets: Reuters reported Meta will begin manufacturing its Iris AI chip in September.
The chip is part of Meta’s MTIA project and is designed with Broadcom and TSMC support.
Meta aims to reduce dependence on Nvidia and AMD as AI compute needs grow.

articles.title: DuckDuckGo browser adds built-in blocking for most YouTube video ads
bullets: DuckDuckGo said its browser can now block most video ads, including on YouTube.
The feature works inside the DuckDuckGo browser rather than the YouTube app.
The move challenges Google’s approach to ads and ad-blocking on video.
